name: Spanakopita Quiche
recipeIngredient: 10 oz Frozen chopped spinach (thawed and drained)
recipeInstructions:
1. Coat a 9-inch pie plate with cooking spray; set aside.
2. Press spinach between paper towels until barely moist; set aside.
3. In a food processor, combine cottage cheese and the next five ingredients. Process until smooth. Add spinach, green onions, oregano, and garlic; process for an additional 45 seconds. Set aside.
4. Working with one phyllo sheet at a time, lightly coat each sheet with cooking spray. Fold the phyllo sheet in half crosswise to form a 13- x 8-1/2-inch rectangle, and lightly coat both sides of the rectangle with cooking spray. Gently press the rectangle into the pie plate, allowing the ends to extend over the edges of the pie plate.
5. Repeat the procedure with a second sheet of phyllo, placing it across the first sheet in a crisscross design. Sprinkle 1 tablespoon breadcrumbs over the second sheet.
6. Repeat the procedure with the remaining phyllo and breadcrumbs, continuing in a crisscross design, ending with phyllo. Fold in the edges to fit the pie plate and form a rim.
7. Pour the spinach mixture into the prepared crust; gently arrange tomato slices over the filling.
8. Bake at 350°F for 55 minutes or until a knife inserted 1 inch from the center comes out clean. Let stand for 10 minutes.
Yield: 6 servings (serving size: 1 wedge)
Serving Ideas: Cut into wedges.
